items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 29 at 5:50AM EDT by NWS Albany NY
items.summary:
A dry but breezy day is forecast today, with relative humidities
this afternoon expected to drop into the 25 to 35 percent range
and southwesterly wind gusts between 20 and 25 mph. When combined
with the lack of recent significant rainfall, elevated fire
weather potential will exist today.

Use caution when handling and disposing of ignition sources such
as matches and cigarettes.
items.area: Northern Litchfield; Southern Litchfield

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 29 at 5:50AM EDT by NWS Albany NY
items.summary:
A dry but breezy day is forecast today, with relative humidities
this afternoon expected to drop into the 25 to 35 percent range.
In addition, southwesterly wind gusts between 20 and 30 mph
develop by this afternoon. When combined with the lack of recent
significant rainfall, elevated fire weather potential will exist
today for areas where the snowpack has completely melted.

Use caution when handling and disposing of ignition sources such
as matches and cigarettes.
items.area: Northern Berkshire; Southern Berkshire

items.summary:
An approaching low pressure system will bring the potential for a
wintry mix of snow and light freezing rain to northwest and north
central North Dakota from late this evening through Monday
morning. The highest probability for light snow accumulations will
be along the International Border, but light snow accumulations
are possible south to the Highway 2 corridor. Temperatures will
have a big impact on travel impacts, but plan for slippery spots
on roads and sidewalks late tonight and through t

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 29 at 4:55AM EDT by NWS Jackson KY
items.summary:
Low humidity, gusty winds, and very dry fuels will combine to
result in High Fire Danger between 10 AM and around sunset today.

A burn ban is currently in place for McCreary County. Check for
any other county burn bans or restrictions that may be in place.

Remember, Kentucky regulations make it illegal to burn between
the hours of 6 AM and 6 PM within 150 feet of any woodlands or
brushlands during the Spring Forest Fire Season that runs until
April 30th.

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 29 at 3:25AM CDT by NWS Grand Forks ND
items.summary:
Low pressure system will be moving into southern Saskatchewan
tonight and east into southern Manitoba Monday. Precipitation
ahead of the system may affect parts of northeast North Dakota and
far northwest Minnesota between midnight and noon Monday and
temperatures may be cold enough for light snow or light freezing
rain. The chances of this is highest along the Manitoba border,
but chances do exist down to Highway 2. Temperatures will be
critical and will determine any travel impacts Monday morn
items.area: West Polk; Kittson; Roseau; West Marshall; East Marshall; Pennington; Red Lake; Towner; Cavalier; Pembina; Benson; Ramsey; Eastern Walsh County; Nelson; Grand Forks; Western Walsh County

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 29 at 12:17AM EDT by NWS Upton NY
items.summary:
Dry and breezy conditions expected today. The combination of
afternoon RH values around 30 to 40 percent and a southwesterly
flow increasing to 20 to 25 mph gusts will result in an elevated
risk of fire spread.

Use caution when handling any potential ignition sources,
including machinery, cigarettes and matches. Any fires that ignite
will have the potential to spread quickly.

The forecast considers meteorological, fuel and land conditions
and has been developed in coordination with state fire
items.area: Northern Fairfield; Northern New Haven; Northern Middlesex; Northern New London; Southern Fairfield; Southern New Haven; Southern Middlesex; Southern New London

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 28 at 10:25PM EDT by NWS Columbia SC
items.summary:
Continued dry fuels and low humidity across the region raise
concern for increased fire danger. Although winds will not be as
strong as the previous day, relative humidity values nearing
critical levels combined with dry fuels support a continued
elevated fire weather threat across the Midlands and CSRA. Outdoor
fires will have the potential to spread quickly, increasing the
danger of wildfires.

A statewide burn ban remains in effect for South Carolina until
further notice.

items.title: Winter Weather Advisory issued March 28 at 8:05PM MDT until March 30 at 10:00AM MDT by NWS Great Falls MT
items.summary:
* WHAT...Snow expected. Total snow accumulations of 2 to 4 inches
across lower elevations, with 4 to 8 inches near Marias Pass and
up to 15 inches over the highest peaks of Glacier National Park.
Winds gusting as high as 50 mph, with localized gusts up to 65 mph.

* WHERE...East Glacier Park Region Zone.

* WHEN...From 8 PM Sunday to 10 AM MDT Monday.

* IMPACTS...Travel could be difficult, with reductions in visibility
due to blowing snow. The Monday morning commute will be impacted.

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 28 at 10:04PM EDT by NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC
items.summary:
While moisture is expected to begin slowly recovering tomorrow,
another very dry afternoon is expected with relative humidity
between 20 and 30 percent forecast across much of the area.
Although winds will be mostly light...between 5 and 10 mph, the
dry air combined with very dry vegetation will result in a
continuation of elevated fire danger.

South Carolina until further notice. Open burning of any kind is
subject to fines until th

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued March 28 at 8:30PM EDT by NWS Peachtree City GA
items.summary:
HIGH FIRE DANGER CONDITIONS SUNDAY MORNING INTO THE EVENING FOR
MUCH OF NORTH GEORGIA DUE TO LOW RELATIVE HUMIDITIES...

Relative Humidities of 30 percent or less can be expected for 4
or more hours Sunday. Winds will be Southeast at 8 to 13 MPH, with
gusts up to 20 MPH.

With extremely dry fuels, high fire danger conditions can be
expected.

Please refer to your local burn permitting authorities
whether you may burn outdoors.  If you do burn outside,
use extreme caution.
